L1.area: Scripting language runtime and expression evaluation

L2.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists where in-place mutation optimizations for data transformation operations use unsafe memory conversion to treat immutable references as mutable. When a variable is both the source and destination of such an operation, references to freed data can persist, allowing access to deallocated memory through stale keys or values.

L3.area: Mutable expression interpreter and its data assignment optimization paths within the scripting engine
L3.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists where in-place mutation optimizations for patch and merge operations on scripting language data structures use unsafe memory transmutation to convert immutable references to mutable ones. When a variable is both the target of such an operation and the assignment destination, references from event data can be inserted into persistent state. After the event data is freed, those references become dangling pointers, allowing access to deallocated memory.

ground_truth.reason: The patch_in_place and merge_in_place functions use unsafe transmute to convert immutable references to mutable ones for in-place optimization, which can cause dangling pointers when event data is freed after references to it have been inserted into persistent state.
